item.g:title: Preloved Hardcover The Comedians
item.g:description: Set in Duvalier’s Haiti, three men navigate a landscape of political terror. The "comedians" wear masks of cynicism or idealism to survive the regime. Brown, Smith, and Jones face moral choices amidst the brutal Tonton Macoute. Greene explores the thin line between playing a part and taking a stand.

item.g:title: Preloved Hardcover The story of the Treasure seekers
item.g:description: The Bastable children seek to restore their fallen family fortune. Through creative schemes, they dig for gold and hunt for treasure. Their humorous failures and imaginative games lead to real-life lessons. It is a charming classic about childhood innocence and sibling loyalty.

item.g:title: Preloved Hardcover Follyfoot
item.g:description: Steve and Dora find a sanctuary for old and unwanted horses. They battle to save neglected animals from cruelty and hard work. The farm becomes a place of healing for both horses and humans. It is a touching story of compassion, resilience, and rural life

item.g:title: Preloved Hardcover The Swiss Family
item.g:description: A shipwrecked family survives on a remote and tropical island. They build an elaborate treehouse and use their many skills. Working together, they tame animals and cultivate the land. It is a classic tale of ingenuity, nature, and family unity.

item.g:title: Preloved Hardcover A tale of two cities
item.g:description: Set in London and Paris, it depicts the French Revolution's chaos. Two men, Darnay and Carton, are bound by love and sacrifice. The guillotine casts a shadow over themes of rebirth and fate. It is a timeless story of redemption through an ultimate act.

item.g:title: Preloved Hardcover The Adventures of Tom Sawyer
item.g:description: Tom Sawyer leads a life of mischief and imagination in Missouri. He ducks chores, hunts for treasure, and explores a dark cave. With Huckleberry Finn, he witnesses a crime and finds true bravery. It is a nostalgic tale of childhood freedom and Southern wit.

item.g:title: Preloved Hardcover Three Men in a Boat
item.g:description: Three friends and a dog row down the scenic Thames. They encounter hilarious mishaps and chaotic camping blunders. The boat journey is filled with witty and timeless humor. It is a classic celebration of Victorian leisure and friendship.

item.g:title: Preloved Hardcover The Railway Children
item.g:description: Three children move to a cottage near a bustling railway. They face their father’s absence with courage and kindness. The tracks bring them new friends and a daring rescue. It is a heartwarming tale of family, hope, and steam.

item.g:title: Preloved Hardcover Oliver Twist
item.g:description: A young orphan escapes a cruel workhouse for London’s streets. He falls in with Fagin’s gang of skilled pickpockets and thieves. Amidst shadows and crime, he seeks his true identity and home. It is a stark tale of social injustice and ultimate mercy

item.g:title: Preloved Hardcover The Wicked Wit of Winston Churchill
item.g:description: A curated collection of the Prime Minister’s sharpest remarks. It showcases his legendary insults, dry humor, and quick repartee. From political debates to social snubs, his genius is revealed. It is a brilliant tribute to a master of the English language

item.g:title: Preloved Hardcover The Prisoner of Zenda
item.g:description: An English traveler is a double for the King of Ruritania. He must impersonate the monarch to save the throne from rebels. High-stakes political intrigue meets a bittersweet and tragic romance. It is a classic adventure of honor, swordfights, and duty.

item.g:title: Preloved The Vanished Man Hardcover
item.g:description: A master illusionist uses deadly magic to baffle the police. Lincoln Rhyme must outsmart a killer who mimics great escapes. The investigation twists through a world of mirrors and traps. It is a high-stakes thriller where nothing is as it seems.

item.g:title: Preloved Hardcover Death on the Nile
item.g:description: A wealthy heiress is murdered during her Egyptian honeymoon cruise. Hercule Poirot must find the killer among a boatload of suspects. Jealousy, greed, and hidden motives surface along the ancient Nile. It is a brilliant mystery of cold hearts and desert heat

item.g:title: Preloved Hardcover The Boys' Book 2: How to Be the Best at Everything Again
item.g:description: It offers a fresh collection of essential skills and stunts. From surviving a disaster to mastering the ultimate prank. It empowers boys with confidence, humor, and practical knowledge. It is a fun-filled guide to becoming a modern-day expert.

item.g:title: Preloved Hardcover The Mystery of the Blue Train
item.g:description: A wealthy heiress is murdered for her priceless rubies. Hercule Poirot boards the luxury train to find the killer. Secrets and false identities unravel along the French Riviera. It is a classic mystery of greed, betrayal, and deduction

item.g:title: Preloved Hardcover The Water Babies
item.g:description: The Water Babies by Charles Kingsley is a classic Victorian fairy tale following Tom, a mistreated young chimney sweep who drowns and is transformed into a tiny underwater "water-baby." As Tom explores a magical aquatic realm filled with talking creatures and moral fairies, he undergoes a spiritual journey to earn his way back to human form. The book serves as a satirical commentary on the harsh social conditions of the Industrial Revolution and early theories of evolution. Ultimately, the story is a celebrated work of children's literature that weaves together fantasy, science, and the importance of ethical growth.

item.g:title: Preloved Hardcover Diary
item.g:description: Samuel Pepys's diary offers an unparalleled, first-hand account of life in 17th-century London between 1660 and 1669. It captures major historical milestones with vivid detail, including the Restoration of the monarchy, the Great Plague, and the Great Fire of London. Beyond politics, Pepys records his personal flaws, daily habits, and private scandals with a candid honesty rarely seen in historical documents. Written in a secret shorthand, the journal remains a cornerstone of English literature for its unique blend of public history and intimate memoir.

item.g:title: The Stationery Shop of Tehran by Marjan Kamali
item.g:description: 1953, Tehran . In a small shop in a country on the brink of unrest, two people meet for the very first time. Roya loves nothing better than to while away the hours in the stationery shop run by Mr Fakhri. The store, stocked with fountain pens, shiny ink bottles, and thick wads of writing paper, also carries translations of literature from all over the world. Bahman, with his burning passion for justice, is like no one else she has ever met. But all around them, as their relationship blossoms, life in Tehran is changing. Suddenly, shockingly, violence erupts: a coup d'etat that forever changes their country's future, as well as their own. Marjan Kamali's beautiful novel explores themes of love and loss, and delivers and unforgettable ending.

item.g:description: Only the extraordinary belong in the kingdom of Ilya . . . The exceptional. The Elites . The Elites have possessed powers for decades, gifted to them by the Plague , while those born Ordinary are just that, banished from the kingdom and shunned from society. No one knows this better than Paedyn Gray, an Ordinary posing as an Elite. When she unsuspectingly saves one of Ilya’s princes, Kai Azer, she’s thrown into the Purging Trials , a brutal competition to showcase her ‘Elite’ powers. If the Trials and the opponents don’t kill her, the prince she’s fighting feelings for will if he discovers what Paedyn really is . . . completely Ordinary.

When she finds an affordable apartment in a beautiful neighbourhood, she knows there must be a catch. Of course, her new roommate Frederick J. Fitzwilliam is far from normal. He sleeps all day, is out at night on business, and talks like he walked out of a regency romance novel. He is also leaves Cassie heart-melting notes around the apartment, always asks about her day, and doesn't look half bad shirtless on the rare occasions they're both home and awake. There's no denying there's a spark between them - but there's also a secret. . . With true love at stake, will Frederick come clean?

Immediately she is mistaken for one of the wedding people - but she's actually the only guest at the Cornwall Inn who isn't here for the big event. Phoebe has dreamed of coming here for years. She hoped to shuck oysters and take sunset sails with her husband but now she is divorced and depressed, and not sure how to go on. She's not been sure how to do anything , lately, except climb into bed and drink gin and tonics and listen to the sound of the refrigerator making ice. Back in District 12, Haymitch Abernathy is trying not to think too hard about his chances. All he cares about is making it through the day and being with the girl he loves. When Haymitch’s name is called, he can feel all his dreams break.
